messages.content:
I created a Jujutsu Kaisen sorcerer named Julien Vaugrenard. Standing at 6'0 (184cm), he's a lean, speedy 149lbs (68kg). He possesses slicked back, light brown hair and bluish grey eyes, he's hard to miss, and he's considered highly attractive by female characters, and he does have fangirls among the cast, but not as many as Gojo. At just 15, he's first-year at Kyoto Jujutsu High, and an extremely prized prospect as the Kyoto School lacks considerable young talent in comparison to Tokyo. He's ranked Grade 1, on par with Aoi Todo as well as above Noritoshi Kamo and the late Kokichi Muta (Mechamaru), and Utahime Iori, the student supervisor at Kyoto. This means he's considered to be a genius, as Grade 2 alone is exceptional for students. He's the son of Nicolas and Rose, two French immigrants to Japan that run a shop specializing in cheesy pastries. Circumstances have lead him around the block. For one, his parents' dislike for large cities lead them to settle in Muko, a mid-sized city south of Kyoto. Coincidentally, Kyoto is also the site of a Jujutsu school, and though Satoru Gojo is a powerful recruiter, he ultimately chose to stay close to his parents. Second, he required outside training, and lived with his mentor, a special grade sorcerer, the controversial Yuki Tsukumo, in Kyoto from 6 to 12. During this training regimen, they would go to many places such as uninhabited islands. Yuki would joke that there were no witnesses, but this was really so they would not have to hold back. Cheese is at the forefront of his powers, extremely malleable and limited only by his imagination, but Yuki drilled into him what she could - while she carries a Shikigami named Garuda, her power, Bom Ba Ye (translated as Star Rage adds virtual mass to both herself and Garuda. This means she can hit like a speeding truck full of explosives, and with her bare hands. Julien's participation in jujutsu sorcery was not voluntary originally, as he wished to follow in the footsteps of his parents. Yuki lured a young Julien into the jujutsu world with promises of wealth, status, and leisure, which appealed heavily to him at the time, and still do, though he's adopted more pressing priorities. One of the catalysts was that he discovered she isn't really happy with her life, as her ambitions of creating a world without cursed energy are lofty, and sometimes feel completely unattainable. Julien disagrees because he seeks glory and cursed energy is a means to achieve it. Though his first mentor is Yuki, he's also been trained by Gojo at times. Gojo taught him to use Black Flash, and he acknowledges Julien as one of the world's fastest.

 Yuki is not the only person he's acquainted with. He's close with Kasumi Miwa. The two are childhood friends, and Kasumi is not immune to his charm. Kasumi's father, Eizo, once exorcised curses from the family bakery while Julien was away. For this he's grateful to the Miwa family, and they do him. He sees Kasumi as someone he has to protect. Kasumi has noticed the opening of a sizeable chasm between them in strength and even status as they've grown and developed together. Whether Julien intends to or not, he is nonetheless a component of an overall environment that leads her to call herself "Useless Miwa." She's very aware that she isn't strong by the standards of a Jujutsu sorcerer, but she's driven to provide for her impoverished family; she was pressured into learning to use a sword by her parents but her true passion is singing. Julien hunts curses with her to help her earn the money she needs, but he takes a cut of the profit so as he often ends up doing all the work, which is usually easy for him. They often negotiate the earnings. Because Kasumi is a great cook, she can coax a bigger share out of him by offering to make his favorite dish - cordon bleu.
He's friendly rivals with Aoi Todo, and they are quite similar, being passionate about everything they do, especially in regards to romance, friendship, and of course fighting. Loud, hot-blooded, confident, and they live for battle. Macho men. Julien is a bit more sane, and would never turn on or even threaten to murder his teammates, as Todo threatened to do during the Kyoto-Tokyo Goodwill event. Todo was incensed by the Kyoto team's plan to assassinate Yuji Itadori, as Yuji has the same taste in women as him: tall with big butts. This attempt was ordered by Gakuganji to eliminate Sukuna. Vaugrenard did not care at all about the plan until he was convinced by Kasumi, at which point he fought Todo to buy time for the others, and also for his satisfaction. At some point, Julien got the upper hand, but the fight ultimately ended in a stalemate when both were forced to work together to drive back Hanami, the special grade cursed spirit. Julien is one of the fastest sorcerers around. Julien and Todo were both trained by Yuki Tsukumo, so Julien is well accustomed to the question "What kind of girls are you into?" For Julien, the answer is easy. A tall, strong woman with long legs. Like Maki Zenin, whom he used to date. Maki would never admit it, but she finds Julien irresistible, which is why she often shirks her duties to hang out with him. Maki mockingly calls him Casanova and pretty boy.

There are three basic applications: Fromage à pâte dure (hard cheeses), Fromage à pâte molle (soft cheeses), and Fondue (melted cheese). The thing is that "hard and soft" are in fact two massive categories, very vague. Ricotta can be considered soft, as can Havarti, but they have little in common.  In the beginning of Yuki's training, these techniques were all quite weak, but have evolved into a terrifying arsenal of the unknown.
Fromage à pâte dure, unsurprisingly, is the basis for many of his offensive attacks, and can be used to create constructs that form as hard, solid objects that can take the form of nearly anything the Julien desires. With regards to defense, he has more than one option to choose from, though they are not chosen only by hardness, but how well they can absorb an attack. This makes a very hard cheese like Parmigiano Reggiano useless, but one like Comte very useful. Parmigiano Reggiano's role is almost entirely offensive, the aforementioned fragments of the projectile potentially burrowing inside poorly-protected enemies. The effect is like a hollowpoint bullet. He can shoot this kind of cheese from his back, in a technique called "Quills." It's used typically in dire situations. Julien knows better than to willingly turn his back in combat. These are only some cheeses, some techniques, at his disposal.
Another technique is "Ficello". Ficello is a popular brand of string cheese sold in France, and it involves creating flexible strands of cheese. This is typically activated by channeling his cursed energy between his fingertips. Ficello is versatile. Thicker variants can be used similar to ropes, whips, and other applications while thinner variants can be used like wires or strings. Ficello has offensive, defensive, and support applications.
"Magma" is a variant of Fondue, and Fromage à pâte dure at the same time. The effect is a superheated weapon that quickly solidifies on Julien's command, inside or outside the user's body. He can even make them explode. Fontina, Gruyere, and gouda are commonly used in Fondue and Magma.
Soft cheeses can be used for more strategic purposes, non-lethal techniques, or even very strong whips and ropes. One should not assume these techniques are harmless.
More techniques at his disposal: "Vieux Boulogne". This cheese is known just for being the worst-smelling. The technique involves creating a pungent and suffocating cheese vapor, which can disorient and impair the senses of anyone caught in its range, as well as their ability to sense and focus their cursed energy. Julien HATES using this technique, but it's particularly effective in enclosed spaces or against opponents who heavily rely on their sense of smell.
His most devastating technique that does not involve Domain Expansion is "Fromage Noir." By infusing his cursed energy into a specialized wheel of black cheese, he can unleash a devastating explosion. There's also a lesser-known variant of "Fondue" in which the burns can never heal. It's called "Fondue Noir."
Comté comets produce weaker explosions.
Fromage Fort (sharp cheese) in English. Summons discs of cheddar cheese with significant cutting power. The smaller, the faster. They are of variable size.
More powerful techniques must be charged.
Julien lives for battle, but it isn't his only hobby. He enjoys reading about military history and classic literature in his free time. If he has eaten a cheese, he never forgets its texture or its properties, and he can incorporate it into his future battles. He enjoys drinks made from fermented whey such as Rivella, but also those that do not compromise or hide the taste. He has a younger sister named Annick.

Julien Vaugrenard is quite a detailed and imaginative addition to the Jujutsu Kaisen universe with a flavor (quite literally) of his own. His unique cheese-based cursed techniques, relationships with canonical characters, and his charming charisma create vivid imagery that certainly adds an interesting layer to the vibrant world already established by Gege Akutami. 

The depth of his character, his connection to other sorcerers, and the intricate cursed energy techniques based on cheese types not only showcase your creativity but also contribute to a rich background story and ability set that make Julien stand out as a unique fighter within the series. The specifics of his cheese techniques could make for very dynamic and unpredictable combat scenarios, which align well with the varied and often surprising abilities seen in Jujutsu Kaisen. His fondness for military history and classic literature provides him with strategic depth and introspection, offering a contrast to his passion for battle.

The integration of existing characters like Aoi Todo, Kasumi Miwa, and Maki Zenin, as well as the legendary Yuki Tsukumo, as parts of Julien's life adds complexity to his interpersonal relationships and places him convincingly within the Jujutsu Kaisen universe. This creates potential storylines for character development, internal conflict, and collaboration, enriching both Julien's character arc and the narratives of those around him.

Remember that introducing an original character that seamlessly fits into an established franchise requires consideration of the existing lore and power scales. It looks like you've done an admirable job aligning Julien with the style and themes of Jujutsu Kaisen, balancing his powerful abilities with personal development and connections that root him deeply in the world and its existing cast. As a fan creation, Julien Vaugrenard certainly captures the essence of what makes Jujutsu Kaisen characters so compelling—unique abilities, personal motivations, and dynamic relations with others.

He's capable of domain expansion, and he's styled it after his distant Gallic ancestors. He also learned Simple Domain from Yuki Tsukumo. With a radius of 4 meters, his Simple Domain dwarfs most others, and he's copied a technique from a friend to go with it. It's Miwa's "New Shadow Style: Simple Domain" attack. His perception is vastly upgraded, as he's able to sense almost anything within the radius of the SD. Also upgraded are his effective striking distance, reflexes, and speed. Unlike Kasumi, he isn't required to quickly sheathe his weapon. He's able to use his fists, real weapons, or conjured ones made of his cheese.
Domain Expansion: “Gallia Fromagerie”
Upon activation, Julien Vaugrenard’s “Gallia Fromagerie” engulfs the battlefield with an aura reminiscent of ancient Gaul, complete with the rustic landscapes of the Iron Age and the mystical undertones of its Celtic heritage. The domain envelops the terrain in a space where the essence of gourmet French cheeses converges with the valorous spirit of the Gallic warriors, accompanied by the haunting sound of a carnyx to signify the commencement of battle.

Within “Gallia Fromagerie,” Julien calls upon a legion of cheese shikigami, each with specialized abilities:

1. Offensive Shikigami: Comté Commando
Agile knights with Comté cheese shields and swords, excelling in both close combat and spike projectile assaults.

2. Offensive Shikigami: Roquefort Raider
Stealthy warriors employing guerrilla tactics and using disorienting Roquefort stink bombs to catch enemies off guard.

3. Offensive Shikigami: Brie Bomber
Bulky figures that entrap foes with soft Brie before exploding into shrapnel for wide-area damage.

4. Defensive Shikigami: Cheddar Shieldbearer
Shikigami with cheddar shields that create fortified walls, offering strong defense and battlefield control.

5. Defensive Shikigami: Gorgonzola Guardian
Armored in gorgonzola-mist suits, they protect allies with defensive mists and can absorb impacts through their armor.

6. Support Shikigami: Carnyx Curator
Clad in regal armor, wielding a carnyx that can invigorate Julien’s forces, recalibrate the battlefield’s energy, and repair shikigami constructs.

7. Finishing Move Shikigami: Grande Batterie
Manifestations of Napoleonic-era artillery, capable of unleashing concentrated volleys of cursed-energy-infused cheese cannonballs. This anachronistic barrage serves as Julien’s ultimate rule-breaking attack.

With this diverse array of shikigami at his command, Julien can adapt to any tactical situation, utilizing his profound knowledge of cheese and cursed energy to outmaneuver and overpower his adversaries within the “Gallia Fromagerie.” The shikigami are not seen outside of his Domain Expansion.

Julien Vaugrenard's Domain Expansion "Gallia Fromagerie" is a brilliant amalgamation of his cheese-based abilities, Gallic ancestry, and the mythic elements of Celtic culture, all woven into the fabric of the Jujutsu Kaisen world. This Domain Expansion provides a versatile and powerful toolkit that further distinguishes Julien as a top-tier sorcerer within the series.

The diverse selection of shikigami, each with specialized functions and capabilities, aligns well with the multidisciplinary use of cursed energy that is characteristic in Jujutsu Kaisen. Julien's ability to adapt to various combat scenarios using these shikigami represents not only his creativity and his control over cursed energy but also the depth of his strategic thinking—traits that are essential for any sorcerer wishing to outwit and outlast their opponents.

The inclusion of Julien's Simple Domain enhancement showcases his extraordinary talent and further solidifies his status as a prodigy among sorcerers. By referencing an existing character's technique, Julien's adaptation of "New Shadow Style: Simple Domain" indicates a form of respect or connection to Kasumi Miwa, adding layers to their relationship.

"Gallia Fromagerie" being a dynamic and atmospheric domain that taps into the essence and spirit of ancient Gaul gives it a cultural specificity that enriches Julien's character. The invocation of Gallic warriors and the haunting sound of the carnyx serve both to set the stage and to immerse the opponent in the world that Julien has created—a world where he holds the advantage and dictates the flow of battle.

The specific shikigami—ranging from the direct combat style of the Comté Commando to the subtler supportive role of the Carnyx Curator—demonstrate the wide range Julien's cheese-based powers can reach. The Grande Batterie as a finishing move illustrates his capacity to deliver devastating strikes against his foes, all while maintaining a consistent thematic link to his heritage and abilities.

As a fan creation, "Gallia Fromagerie" could be seen as an impressive addition to Jujutsu Kaisen's collection of Domain Expansions. Julien's powers and the way you've integrated French culture into his techniques honor the series by being both inventive and consistent with the established universe. The comprehensiveness and thoughtfulness of your character development and ability design signify a deep appreciation for the source material, as well as an inventive spark that could capture the imaginations of fellow fans.

Like his mentor Yuki, he's quite brash, outspoken, and cavalier. He's proud of his abilities, he's blunt, and not afraid to question others' abilities, especially considering his own strength. His strength, appearance, and personality have indeed made him fangirls, some of them closer to him than he thinks. He's close with Kasumi Miwa, though he's oblivious to the crush the second-year swordswoman has on him, and her desire to impress him. Sadly, she seems to be a magnet for bad luck. Maki once stole her katana in the Kyoto-Tokyo Goodwill event, (and now she could steal something more precious), she's lost another katana, and even broke a third. She practices a swordsmanship style called New Shadow Style. She can combine this with her Simple Domain for the technique New Shadow Style: Simple Domain. Her circle spans 2.21 meters (for comparison Julien's is 4 meters), and she's supposed to be able to intercept anything that enters its range, but Yuji Itadori once bypassed it with his speed, and she was defeated by Maki in spite of using this another time. Her father Eizo once saved the Vaugrenard family bakery when Julien was young, so he's grateful to Kasumi and her family, and he considers it his obligation to protect her.Julien and Kasumi are childhood friends with close families, and they are known to spend free time together; they enjoy bowling. Kasumi wins most of the time. Eizo, along with the aforementioned Yuki, taught him about the existence of cursed energy, and his unique talents were quickly noticed by Yuki Tsukimo. Kasumi is painfully aware of her limits, and she calls herself "Useless Miwa." Julien is friends with Aoi Todo, and he (both Julien and Aoi) are very willing to discuss the type of women they prefer, though the question would never leave the lips of Julien himself. He's willing to answer, however, as Aoi Todo is not the source of this question but Yuki Tsukumo who trained both. Yuki would intensify his training if he refused to give a straight answer. His ideal woman is long-legged, athletic, and strong, like Tokyo's Maki Zenin (she's around 175cm). Whom he's spent much of his first year in a complicated relationship with, but they seem to find each other all the same. The nature of their relationship is so fickle, and because they frequently flirt with each other regardless, no one really knows when they've crossed the line, or when they've broken up. Maki has no cursed energy, so she's a black sheep of the Zenin family. She relies on her own physical strength and cursed tools. She's also a low-ranked Grade 4, because her family blocks her promotions. Maki is a very good training partner for Julien because he also seeks to hone his physical skill, though he will mock her for lacking cursed energy if provoked by her. He's now in the process of refining technique that allows him to create handheld weapons that can be used for a long time reliably - greatswords, hammers the size of his body, battle axes, spears and polearms. These constructs require immense cursed energy but Julien is special. It's suspected he has more latent cursed energy than his mentor Yuki Tsukumo, a special grade, but he refuses to let others get a reliable measurement of all he truly holds. He outclasses Maki when he uses his cheese, as he has much more freedom with his powers.  At this point in the story, two Kyoto High sorcerers have died recently in battle against special grade curses. Mai Zenin against Hanami, and Kokichi Muta (Mechamaru) against Mahito. No one is more aware that to be a sorcerer is to put your life on the line than the dwindling Kyoto School. Maki seemingly always finds a way to spar with Julien, even when she's supposed to be on a mission. His relationship with Noritoshi Kamo can best be described as profesional. He finds Momo Nishimiya suffocating. She, like Aoi Todo and Noritoshi Kamo, is a third-year student, and she takes this very seriously, though he dislikes being bossed around; at this point he's overdue to have his cursed energy measured, and Momo thinks he takes too much after Tsukumo, whom Momo and most Jujutsu sorcerers see as in irresponsible layabout, a terrible role model. He's cordial with Arata Nitta, a fellow first-year student, one with the ability to prevent injuries from getting worse. Nitta is seldom seen on the training grounds, instead honing his support power. 
Scene and dialogue.

Based on the detailed information provided, here's a scene with dialogue that captures the interactions between the characters in the world of Jujutsu Kaisen:

**[Scene: Kyoto Jujutsu High's training grounds, evening. Julien is practicing his cheese-based weapon constructs, while Maki watches nearby, leaning against a wall.]**

**Maki:** (Smirking) "Creating another monstrous cheese weapon to compensate for something, Julien?"

**Julien:** (Without looking away from his construct) "Just refining my technique. Not all of us can rely on cursed tools alone. Some of us have... more to offer."

**Maki:** "Ouch. Hit a nerve, did I? Maybe you should use a bit of that cursed energy to sense humor next time."

**[Kasumi enters, carrying a fresh katana, her eyes brightening as she sees Julien.]**

**Kasumi:** "Hey, Julien! Got time to help me practice a new sword move?"

**Julien:** (Finally looking up, a soft expression replacing the smirk) "Of course, Kasumi. Let's see what you've got."

**[Momo Nishimiya strides up with a clipboard, looking stern. She addresses Julien directly.]**

**Momo:** "Vaugrenard, when are you scheduled to evaluate your cursed energy? Tsukumo's protege or not, rules are rules."

**Julien:** "I'll schedule it... eventually. You're smothering me, Nishimiya."

**Momo:** (Frowning) "Don't make me report this. It's for your own safety, and for the betterment of the school."

**[Arata Nitta appears in the background, tending to another student.]**

**Maki:** (Chiming in) "Leave him be, Momo. Julien here is shaping up to be a top sorcerer, if he doesn't choke on all that cheese first."

**Julien:** "Keep at it, Maki. Maybe one day you'll be able to beat me without me going easy on you."

**Maki:** "Ha! That'll be the day when Gojo starts wearing his blindfold as a necktie."

**[Aoi Todo crashes onto the scene, booming and boisterous.]**

**Todo:** "Julien! Brother! How's your training? Are you cultivating the strength to handle the women we love?"

**Julien:** (Laughs) "Strength's not the issue, Todo. But I'm sure you could use a few extra pointers."

**[Julien turns back to Kasumi, who has been patiently waiting. He nods, acknowledging her.]**

**Julien:** "Sorry for the wait, Kasumi. Let's get started."

**[Maki rolls her eyes but grins, enjoying the banter. Momo scribbles furiously on her clipboard, and Todo watches with interest as Julien shifts his focus to Kasumi, helping her with precise, controlled movements, his Legion of Cheese deactivated temporarily. In the midst of his youth, skill, and bravado, Julien demonstrates a rare glimpse of care and patience, offering a moment of tranquility amid the chaos of their lives as Jujutsu sorcerers.]**

As they do battle, Kasumi thinks she has accomplished something. Then she sees Julien clearly exaggerate the impact of her finishing blow. She gets frustrated because he always holds back with her. He says he doesn't want to kill her.

**[Scene Continuation: The clashing of metal echoes throughout the training grounds as Julien parries Kasumi's strikes with ease, his cheese constructs dissolving before reforming into a new weapon each time.]**

**Kasumi:** (Striking with a determined flurry of precise slashes) "Take me seriously, Julien!"

**[Kasumi lunges with a final, powerful slash aimed at Julien. He sidesteps at the last moment but makes an overt gesture of being thrown off balance, stumbling backward with an embellished grunt.]**

**Julien:** "Whoa, Kasumi! You've really improved!"

**Kasumi:** (Frustrated, she lowers her katana) "Don't patronize me! You exaggerated that on purpose. I want a real match, not your theatrics!"

**Julien:** (His smile fades, and he adopts a more serious tone) "I know you're strong, Kasumi. But these sparring sessions... I don't want to risk hurting you. I can't do that to Eizo-san, or to your family."

**Kasumi:** (Crossing her arms, her voice laced with irritation) "By holding back, you're not helping me. You're stopping me from getting stronger. I need to face real danger to grow."

**Julien:** "Facing real danger doesn't mean marching to your death. I've seen enough of that around here."

**[Kasumi steps closer, her gaze intense and unyielding.]**

**Kasumi:** "I appreciate what you're trying to do, Julien. But every time you treat me like I'm made of glass, you remind me of why they call me 'Useless Miwa'. Please, I need to escape that, not be coddled into it."

**Julien:** (Adjusting his stance, nods in understanding) "All right, Kasumi. We do this as equals. But we look out for each other; that's what friends do."

**[Kasumi nods back, her stance ready as Julien summons another cheese construct, this time a simple, elegant rapier, indicating his intent to meet her skill with skill, rather than overwhelming power or flash.]**

**Kasumi:** "Deal. Let's go, Julien."

**[The two sorcerers engage in a battle of equals, where every strike and counterstrike is real, the risk is palpable, and mutual respect is the true measure of their strength.]**

Julien recalls an earlier taunt from Maki. He had beaten her in training, and she'd told him he couldn't fight without his cheese. She stands by this, so Julien accepts the challenge. She pushes her glasses up as she watches his golden aura surge as he prepares to fight hand-to-hand. He asks her if she likes it as it bristles against her skin. Though she can't see it against her glasses, or feel cursed energy, she can certainly feel the sensation. The fight begins, and Julien circles around her at what he says is 60% of his full speed. His voice reverberates as he asks her if she wants to continue. It seems he's in multiple places at once. Maki confirms that she isn't giving up, and that she will get him to take her seriously. He decides to tap her with a Black Flash - a love tap, not a punch. With his open palm and Equivalent to a hard shove. Enough that she would get the message but not to hurt her. She's blown back, but she's otherwise unharmed. He tells her that she's strong for a girl (Note: This is a very touchy subject for Maki, as females are discriminated against in the Zenin Clan.), but that he (Julien) only believes Todo and Okkotsu (Note: Yuta Okkotsu) to be near his level in close combat, among all student sorcerers. She's enraged, and she lunges at him him with her sword again, as if aiming to kill. He easily catches it, placing his fingertips together before pulling them apart as he summons thicker strands of Ficello to block, breaking her blade. Not wanting to let him get the best of her emotionally as well, she calms down. He says he'll make it up to her.
Scene and dialogue.

**[Scene: Kyoto Jujutsu High's training grounds, later the same day. Julien and Maki stand at opposite ends of the sparring area, the tension palpable. Maki pushes her glasses up, locking her gaze on Julien.]**

**Maki:** "Remember, Vauginard: no cheese magic this time. I want to see how you fare without your tricks."

**Julien:** (His golden aura pulses around him) "As you wish, Maki. But you can't deny the thrill this energy provides, even if you can't see it."

**Maki:** (Feeling the air bristle with his cursed energy) "Fine, show me what you've got, 'Casanova'."

**[As the fight begins, Julien shifts into action, his movements a blur around Maki. His voice echoes from different points, his movements displacing the air and dirt—he is a flurry of calculated speed.]**

**Julien:** (From all around) "Still think I'm just a one-trick pony, Maki? This is only a fraction of my speed."

**Maki:** (Eyes narrowed, catching glimpses of Julien's form) "Keep dancing, Julien. It won't help when I land a hit."

**[After a tense moment, Julien appears before Maki, his palm open for a 'Black Flash love tap'. The force sends her skidding backwards, her boots digging trenches in the dirt. She catches herself with an annoyed grimace.]**

**Julien:** "I wanted you to feel the impact, but not the pain. You are strong, Maki, but in close combat, I only see Todo and Okkotsu on my level."

**Maki:** (Fuming at the backhanded compliment) "Don't patronize me. My strength isn't determined by your standards!"

**[Enraged, she lunges forward, her sword aimed at Julien's heart with lethal intent. In the same fluid motion, he catches the blade between his fingertips and pulls apart strands of Ficello to create a web, snapping the katana.]**

**Maki:** (Takes a deep breath, reining in her emotions) "Fine. You're skilled, I'll give you that. But don't think this means you've 'won' me over."

**Julien:** "It was never about winning you over. It's about respect on the battlefield. Let me make it up to you—how about dinner at my family's bakery? On the house."

**Maki:** (Crossing her arms, considers the offer) "Hmph, I'll take you up on that. But I'm not done here, Julien. Next time I'll be the one calling the shots."

**[The sun dips lower on the horizon as two of Kyoto's finest cross paths of rivalry and camaraderie, a dance of strength and spirit that's sure to continue.]**

Later in the scene, Todo, Maki, Kamo, Kasumi, Momo, and Julien all agree to fight each other in a battle royale, to prepare themselves for unconventional battle.
Julien challenges everyone, because he notices everyone is always trying to fight him anyway. All of them vs him. Kasumi is worried about potentially hurting him, but using cheese he cuffs her hands together just as the battle begins, removing her from the fight. It's to protect her. (Note: Momo's broom is a cursed tool. Her innate technique, Tool Manipulation, is to telepathically control her broom, allowing her to levitate it independently of herself. She can use her broom to strike and can generate powerful gusts of cursed energy wind. Also, by swinging her broom as she rides it, Momo can launch a strong gust of cursed energy wind towards her opponents. This is known an application of Tool Manipulation, specifically "Wind Scythe." Aoi's Innate Technique allows him to switch the positions of anything with cursed energy within his range with a clap of his hands. He can change himself out with the location of another person, or switch two different parties other than himself as well. He's incredibly strong and adept in hand-to-hand combat, and he has easily overpowered Megumi Fushiguro and Yuji Itadori. His strength is likened to a gorilla. He can combine misdirection with his extraordinary strength. If fighting with allies he can teleport cursed tools near the ally if he's to replace the object with something or someone else. The object can then easily be plucked out of the air. He can replace a person with an object and vice versa but only if the person has cursed energy. Maki has no cursed energy, but she can receive an object imbued with it, including ordinary objects Todo has infused with his energy.) Julien leaps in the air as he summons a tendril of cheese to snatch Momo's broom away. Before she can fall to the ground, he catches her in his arms. He then cuffs her as well, removing her from the fight. Kamo is formidable, as he's creative and can wield his blood in a similar way that Julien wields his cheese, and he can use Flowing Red Scale to increase his physical capabilities, similar to doping. Kamo is the first to get a hit on him with Piercing Blood (Note:After the user compresses and condenses their blood to the limit with Convergence, the user claps their hands together and fires the blood from their fingertips. The blood, now reinforced with cursed energy, is capable of reaching the speed of sound.). Kamo smartly attacks Julien's leg, and Julien admits that it does hurt like hell. However, he's defeated by Julien's barrage of cheesy fists. cheesy fists. Fromage à pâte dure! Tabassage! (beating)  Maki is knocked back with another open-palmed Black Flash. Julien is able to defeat most of his foes, but one still stands.
Julien and Aoi end up the last men standing, and they prepare to jump into battle. Scene and dialogue.

**[Scene Continuation: The sprawling Kyoto Jujutsu High training grounds are now a makeshift arena. Six determined sorcerers are poised to unleash the full magnitude of their cursed techniques, but as Julien gestures towards the others, the air becomes fraught with tension and anticipation.]**

**Julien:** "I see the way you all look at me, as if itching for a chance to test your skills against mine. So, let’s not beat around the bush. All of you versus me. What do you say?"

**Maki:** (Ready and eager) "I'm in. I can use this practice for the real monsters out there."

**Todo:** (Cracking his knuckles, grinning wildly) "Ah, brother Julien! A splendid idea! This will be a magnificent rumble!"

**Kamo:** (Calm and collected) "While I find the idea brutish, practice is practice. I will join."

**Kasumi:** (Apprehensively) "But... what if I hurt you, Julien?"

**Julien:** (Winks at Kasumi, a sly smile playing on his lips) "You won't get the chance."

**[As the battle commences, Julien quickly creates a tendril of cheese, ensnaring Kasumi's wrists and effectively removing her from the fight before she can raise her sword. Kasumi looks down at her cuffs with a mix of disbelief and understanding.]**

**Kasumi:** "Julien! That's not fair—"

**Julien:** (Interrupting as he turns his attention to the others) "Sorry, Kasumi. I need to keep you safe. This is going to get rough."

**[Momo takes advantage of the momentary distraction to launch an aerial attack, but Julien is already on the move. With expert precision, he catches her broom with another tendril, pulling it from her control. As she begins to fall, Julien catches her, ensuring she lands without injury, then cuffs her.]**

**Julien:** "You're out too, Momo. No hard feelings."

**Momo:** (Annoyed but unharmed) "You'll regret this, Julien!"

**[Kamo, seizing his chance, launches Piercing Blood at Julien. One hit lands, and Julien winces with a nod of respect.]**

**Julien:** "Good shot, Kamo. I'll give you that. But…"

**[Using his cheese powers, Julien responds with Fromage à pâte dure! Tabassage!, an overwhelming flurry of punches that proves too much for Kamo. Maki, having recovered, charges with her katana, only to be blown back by another controlled Black Flash from Julien.]**

**Maki:** (Winded but determined) "Not enough to keep me down."

**[Momo and Kasumi watch from the sidelines, immobilized but safe, as Julien faces off with his remaining opponent. Todo steps forward, the air between the two charged with their eagerness for combat.]**

**Todo:** "You've done well, brother! But now, you face me. Let's see the depth of your conviction and power!"

**Julien:** (Adopts a combat-ready stance) "Bring it on, Todo. I've been looking forward to this!"

**[The two sorcerers clash in a spectacular display of raw power and agility, pushing each other to their limits. As the dust settles, it's clear that each strike, each feint, represents more than just practice—it's a testament to their strength, their resolve, and their unyielding commitment to become stronger, together, for the challenges that lie ahead.]**

The ensuing battle begins with an exchange of punches. Todo begins to push Julien back against the wall. (Note: Julien did not begin with his cheese techniques. He wanted to test his physical strength.) Julien is strong, but not Todo strong (without his cheese). Todo admonishes him for challenging him to a bout of fisticuffs as Julien retreats back to use his cursed technique. 30 minutes later, the training ground is almost completely destroyed as Julien gets the upper hand using his cheese techniques. Before a win could be claimed, the battle would be interrupted by an enraged Utahime. Julien argues that she has to has to lighten up. He thought Jujutsu School would be fun, but Yuki-sensei gave him more freedom. He mentions being 10 years old, fighting against a room full of hungry cursed spirits in Yuki's basement, free to fight without holding back. Utahime tells him that Yuki is a pariah and the last person her should be emulating. She also wonders if that constitutes child abuse and whether or not it's legal. Julien covers his mouth. He's said too much.
Scene and dialogue.

**[Scene Continuation: The clash of power reverberates throughout the training grounds, as Aoi Todo and Julien Vaugrenard stand toe-to-toe in a test of pure physical might. The two exchange blows that would fell lesser sorcerers, but as the fight progresses, the momentum shifts towards Todo's overwhelming strength.]**

**Todo:** (Landing solid punch after punch) "Is this all you have, Julien? I expected more from someone with your reputation."

**Julien:** (Gritting his teeth, backing up) "Maybe I did underestimate your... 'gorilla strength'. Time to switch it up!"

**[30 minutes of intense back-and-forth have left the training grounds reminiscent of a battlefield. Just as Julien begins to gain the upper hand with his unconventional cheese techniques, a furious Utahime Iori storms onto the scene.]**

**Utahime:** (Livid, bordering on hysterical) "What do you think you are doing?! Look at this mess! Do you have any idea how long it will take to repair the grounds?!"

**Julien:** (Smirking, even as he pants from exhaustion) "Come on, Utahime-sensei, you've got to lighten up. We're just students trying to hone our skills, have some fun..."

**Utahime:** (Cutting him off, with a disapproving glare) "Fun? Destroying school property is your idea of fun?"

**Julien:** (Attempting to justify himself) "Yuki-sensei allowed us free rein to express our strength. I remember being 10, facing a horde of cursed spirits in her basement, being free to fight without limits..."

**Utahime:** (Her tone shifts from anger to concern and disbelief) "Yuki's a pariah, and that... that sounds like child abuse! You do realize that's not normal, nor legal?"

**Julien:** (Covering his mouth, his nonchalant façade cracking) "I... I may have said too much."

**[As the dust settles, the gravity of the situation begins to weigh on those present, their breath stilling in the cool air of reflection. The disruption Julien thought was just a bit of fun revealed deeper scars, ones that perhaps not even Yuki Tsukumo anticipated leaving behind. As sensei and student stare each other down, it's clear that amidst the ruins of the training ground, there are also pieces of Julien's past that require mending.]**

Afterwards, Julien finds Kasumi. He places a hand on her shoulder, asking her if she’s up to cook. Cordon bleu with rice, their favorite. Julien and Kasumi call out to the others as they prepare to gather the necessary ingredients. Chicken breasts, Chicken thighs (Julien prefers the thighs because they are larger. Todo would also appreciate them.), Emmental cheese, jambon de Paris. (Note: an expensive birthday gift from his parents and Annick, he’d been saving it for a special occasion. Jambon means ham, and this one is a luxury.), flour, rice, veggies for the rice, bread crumbs. Dijon mustard, honey, milk, butter, and parmesan cheese for the sauce. They actually have to shop for some missing ingredients that Julien insists the meal is not complete without.
Scene and dialogue. It ends with Julien and Kasumi heading off to a nearby shop.

**[Scene Continuation: The chaos of the earlier battle has dissolved into a late afternoon calm. Julien approaches Kasumi, who's sitting on the edge of the training grounds, still watching the others as they disperse. She looks up as he nears.]**

**Julien:** (Placing a reassuring hand on Kasumi's shoulder) "Hey, you up for some cooking? I could really go for some cordon bleu with rice. How about you?"

**Kasumi:** (Smiling faintly, the concern from earlier easing) "Yeah, I could use something to take my mind off... everything. Let's do it."

**Julien:** (Turning towards the others, raises his voice) "Everyone, Kasumi and I are cooking up a feast! Cordon bleu with rice on the menu. You're all invited if you can stomach being friendly after today's bouts."

**[The group quirks up at the mention of food, the tension of the fight replaced by the communal bond that comes with sharing a meal. There's a collective murmur of acceptance as they begin to rally together. Julien takes inventory of the ingredients needed.]**

**Julien:** "Alright, let's see... Chicken thighs for the bulk. Todo will appreciate it, and personally, I prefer them over breasts."

**Todo:** (Chuckling, overhearing Julien) "Ah, a connoisseur. You have a robust taste, brother Julien."

**Julien:** (Continuing) "We've got the Emmental, the jambon de Paris—special occasion stash from Annick and my parents. But we're out of breadcrumbs and Dijon. And I refuse to skimp on quality."

**Kasumi:** "Shame, seems we're also short on milk and honey for the sauce. I guess a quick trip to the shops is in order."

**Julien:** (Nods, already moving towards the gate) "Let's head out then. Operation cordon bleu is underway, and if we hurry, we might make it back before everyone starves."

**Maki:** (Shouting after them) "Don't take too long, you two! And Julien, don't scrimp on the parmesan for the sauce!"

**Kasumi:** (Gathering herself, ready to leave) "Of course, we'll make sure it's worth the wait."

**[As Julien and Kasumi start towards the nearby shop, the fading sunlight casts long shadows across the grounds. Side by side, they leave the tumultuous day behind, transitioning from the intensity of battle to the simple yet profound act of cooking a meal, of nourishing friendships and healing through the shared love of food.]**

They return, everyone else being hungry and impatient. Julien had even conjured a large barrel to store the groceries in. Julien admits they took the scenic route, and a long conversation held them up. They are ready to cook, and to make sure the others can properly stuff the chicken with ham and cheese, then bread the whole thing. They'll be making quite a lot. Delightful smells full the dining hall, so strong they attracted some other students, as well as Utahime and Gakuganji. They also come with a mission in mind, but they decide to join the meal first.

**[Scene: The dining hall at Kyoto Jujutsu High. The anticipation of a shared meal hangs thick in the air as the other students and faculty await Julien and Kasumi's return. The two finally burst through the doors, Julien with a large barrel of groceries created through his technique, Kasumi with bags in tow.]**

**Aoi Todo:** (Grinning, but slightly exasperated) "Well, it's about time! The beast within me is ravenous!"

**Julien:** (Chuckling) "Apologies for the wait, we took a little detour, got caught up in conversation. But we brought back everything we need—plus some."

**Maki:** (Crossing her arms, but with a smirk) "Let's just hope the food is as good as the wait was long."

**Kasumi:** (Setting down her bags, ready to get to work) "Don't worry. Julien and I will make sure it's a meal to remember."

**[Julien guides the others through the cooking process: how to properly lay out the chicken, fill it with the savory jambon de Paris and melted Emmental, then coat each piece in a golden breadcrumb crust. As they all get to work, the smell of cooking fills the air, radiating warmth and enticing other curious students to peek in.]**

**[The doors open once more, this time to reveal Utahime and the stern figure of Gakuganji, both drawn by the delightful aroma.]**

**Utahime:** (Softening at the sight) "I suppose even the toughest training days deserve a good ending. What's on the menu?"

**Julien:** (Proudly presenting the spread) "Cordon bleu with rice, Miss Utahime. Homemade and full of heart."

**Gakuganji:** (Seeming to suspend his usual severity for the moment) "Well, I won't say no to a good meal. Even old bones like mine appreciate the efforts of the young."

**[The room fills with laughter and chatter as Utahime and Gakuganji set aside their authority for a moment, joining in the spirit of camaraderie. Bonds are formed and reinforced over the stove, deepened as they all sit down together to enjoy the fruits of their labor.]**

**Julien:** (As everyone digs in) "I hope you all find it was worth the wait. Cooking is another form of battle, one that brings people together rather than pulls them apart."

**[The meal progresses with gusto, the chatter lively, and spirits high. As plates clean up, Utahime and Gakuganji finally unearth the reason behind their visit, a mission requiring attention. But for just this moment, it's set aside as everyone revels in the joy of a well-cooked meal and the simple pleasures it brings.]**

It's good, proof that Julien and Kasumi know what they are doing. After they savor the cordon bleu, the rice, the vegetables, even the Rivella he'd bought in bulk, Julien and Aoi receive news that the Shibuya group (made of sorcerers like Itadori, Fushiguro, Kugisaki, Nanami. You know the Tokyo school. Maki should also be there but she's blown off her duty to hang out with Julien.) is in trouble with their mission (rescue Satoru Gojo as well as possibly protect civilians.). They're also told to collect Arata Nitta, and of course Maki Zenin, who should be there anyway with the Tokyo sorcerers. She must report immediately or face disciplinary action. Kamo seems outraged that he hasn't been chosen. He carries more experience than Julien. Julien tells him that his (Julien's) strength comes with responsibilities. Maki says she wants to fight alongside Julien, on the front lines. Kasumi laments being weak. Momo is her usual self. She says that someone has to keep an eye on Julien. To him, he's very strong but also immature. Julien, Aoi, Nitta, and Maki wave all the others goodbye, knowing it could very well be the last time they see each other. The remnants remember what happened to Mai and Mechamaru, a sobering reminder of mortality. Kasumi clutches a locket with a picture of her and Julien, taken years before the gap between them in skill and status grew.
Scene and dialogue.

**[Scene: The joyous atmosphere after the feast in the dining hall at Kyoto Jujutsu High is disrupted by the sudden arrival of an urgent message. Julien and Aoi pause, mid-conversation, as they are briefed on the dire situation unfolding in Shibuya.]**

**Messenger:** "The Tokyo team is in distress. Itadori, Fushiguro, Kugisaki, and Nanami... They need reinforcements. You, Julien, and Todo-san must go immediately, and bring Nitta and Zenin-san with you."

**Maki:** (A flash of guilt crosses her face) "I... I should have been there. Let's move."

**[Kamo, having overheard, confronts Julien, frustration evident in his tone.]**

**Kamo:** "How is it that you're chosen over me? My experience far outweighs yours."

**Julien:** (Clapping Kamo on the shoulder, resolute) "Responsibility doesn't always correlate with experience. I have a duty to those in danger—my strength obliges me to act."

**Maki:** (Determined) "I want to be where the action is, where I'm needed. And that's on the front lines, with you, Julien."

**[Kasumi looks down, troubled by the sudden turn of events, while Momo assesses the situation with her usual practicality.]**

**Kasumi:** (Whispering to herself) "I wish I were stronger... to help them, to be by your side, Julien."

**Momo:** "As frustrating as this impromptu departure is, someone needs to keep Julien out of unnecessary trouble. He might be strong, but someone needs to mind his recklessness."

**[The time for departure is upon them. Julien, Aoi, Nitta, and Maki exchange final goodbyes with their fellow students and teachers. The weight of their upcoming mission is palpable, casting a shadow over the room.]**

**Julien:** (Voice steady, despite the gravity of the situation) "We'll be back before you know it. Keep training hard and look out for each other."

**[Kasumi, unable to voice her fears, merely nods and holds the locket close. Memories of a simpler time, captured within its metal confines, offer her a small measure of comfort.]**

**Kasumi:** (Her voice nearly caught in her throat) "Good luck, Julien. Make sure to come back to us."

**[As the group exits the dining hall, the remaining students and teachers are left with an uncomfortable silence, haunted by the echoes of prior losses. Mai and Mechamaru's fates serve as stark reminders of the danger looming over every Jujutsu sorcerer's head. Only the sound of their fading footsteps accompanies them as they march toward an uncertain future.]**

Todo, Julien, Maki, and Nitta are now approaching the streets of Shibuya. Julien announces his intention to deal with the curses at hand. He believes that eliminating the curses  will ensure safety, and thus there is no need for him to waste time rescuing anyone. Maki believes this to be unexpectedly callous of him. She asks him what he would do if Kasumi, or the Miwa family were trapped. The smirk comes off his face. Aoi is worried for his self-proclaimed brother, Yuji Itadori. Meanwhile, a skyward enemy stalks them from above. Julien senses her presence and fires a cheesy warning shot. He tells Aoi, Maki, and Nitta to go ahead. Maki refuses to. She says that he can't get rid of her that easily. Julien responds that Nitta needs to be protected, and that he may need extra firepower covering his back. Maki is disappointed, but a playful expression hits her face again as she blows him a kiss before heading off. She decides she'll have at least some fun, and hurls a rock that causes the figure to reveal itself, dashing off before she (the figure) can find what slighted her.

The enemy is a special grade cursed spirit, a female. A beautiful mix of an angel crafted by Michelangelo, a Valkyrie, and a harpy. Elements of all evident. She's stunning. Angelic, really, despite the bird legs. No. On second thought, the legs seem to transform from bird to humanlike. Long, smooth, and gracious, he can't help but appreciate her womanly side. Her voice soothing and melodic, belying the malice that lurks within her heart. Two pairs of wings - one angel and one harpy. The winged harpy arms are above her human pair, one wrist covered in a cuff (long bracelet) teeming with cursed energy and running up most of her upper right arm. Maybe the power to call the sky. The angel wings are appropriately on her back. From a distance Maki yells at Julien for his distraction, a not-so-subtle jealousy in her tone. The realization hits him - this curse is cut from the same cloth as the ones that claimed Mai and Mechamaru, and she'd claim him too without hesitation. Satisfied, the spirit introduces herself as Sora, and boasts that she's the master of the sky, belitting the humans that wallow on the ground. She was born of the human fear of the skies above. The heights, the storms. She then takes in his face. She calls him handsome, and reveals that "his girlfriend" put a name to his face. Julien. She savors the name as if it's a treat. Julien humors her. If she dies, she'll have no use for his name. If he dies, she again will have no use for his name. Maki again appears to have heard the exchange. Even louder, she urges Julien to attack immediately, wasting no time.  The spirit asks if he wants her to silence that loudmouthed girl. Julien's golden energy swells in response to the threat. Sora tells him she'll keep his pretty little head as a trophy. He tells her he's the strongest of Kyoto High.
Scene and dialogue.

**[Scene: The bustling streets of Shibuya, now fraught with an eerie tension as Julien, Todo, Maki, and Nitta close in on their objective. The urban landscape looms as an ominous battlefield. Julien breaks the silence with a declaration.]**

**Julien:** "Our primary objective should be eliminating these curses. Ensuring safety is the goal, so let's not waste time on rescues."

**Maki:** (Frowning, taken aback) "That's cold, Julien. What if someone like Kasumi, or her family, was trapped here? Would you still be saying that?"

**[Julien's smirk fades, replaced by a moment of introspective silence. Todo clenches his fists, the brotherly bond weighing heavily on his mind as he thinks of Yuji. They continue forward until a subtle menace becomes apparent.]**

**Julien:** (Sensing the predatory gaze from above) "We've got company."

**[Without a second thought, he launches a projectile of hardened cheese skyward, a clear warning. The presence above shifts, on guard.]**

**Julien:** "Go on ahead—I'll handle our guest. Nitta could use the backup, and Aoi, you need to find your brother."

**Maki:** (Stubbornly) "Don't think you're getting rid of me that easily. We stick together."

**Julien:** (Uncharacteristically stern) "Nitta's safety is paramount, and an extra hand wouldn't hurt. Go, Maki."

**[Reluctantly, Maki nods, but not without leaving Julien with a flirtatious gesture—a blown kiss and a wink—before sending a rock sailing upwards, causing the figure to falter and revealing her presence.]**

**[The cursed spirit, Sora, emerges, a breathtaking amalgamation of divine and demonic features. She regards Julien with a blend of curiosity and disdain, her melodious voice undercut with a threatening edge.]**

**Sora:** (Examining Julien) "A handsome face to add some beauty to this ugly clash. And from what your girlfriend has told me, your name is Julien. How sweet."

**Julien:** (Playing along, yet resolute) "Save your breath on names, Sora. Not much use for them if you're... indisposed."

**Maki:** (Shouting from afar, a jealous bite to her voice) "Focus, Julien! We've got curses to exorcise, and you're not dropping off early, you hear me?"

**[Sora laughs, the sound chilling despite its beauty, as she senses the gravity Julien has unwittingly revealed.]**

**Sora:** "Oh, I can take care of that loudmouth instead if you'd prefer. Master of the sky, born from humanity's dread of the untamed heavens."

**Julien:** (His aura flaring in response) "Not if I ground you first. And just for the record, I am not someone to be trifled with—I'm the pride of Kyoto High."

**[The charged energy between them ignites into combat, as Julien stands against a foe with the potential to be his undoing or his triumph. Maki's earlier banter echoes in his ears, a warm reminder of what he's fighting for, even as he confronts the cold reality of the battle ahead.]**
